title: Ground equipment problem scrubs Starship launch attempt
summary: SpaceX canceled the first attempt to launch the latest version of its Starship vehicle on May 21 due to a ground equipment issue. The scrub highlights challenges in preparing the massive rocket for its maiden flight.

title: Sixth Varda mission successfully returns
summary: Varda Space Industries completed its sixth reentry mission on May 18, focusing on pharmaceutical research and hypersonic testing. The company continues to advance its space-based manufacturing and reentry capabilities.

title: Space Force on path to double active-duty force by 2030
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to double its active-duty force by 2030, but growth is limited by training capacity and the establishment of new operational units. General Chance Saltzman highlighted these constraints during the announcement.

title: Starship underpins SpaceX’s growth ambitions
summary: SpaceX's upcoming Starship test flight is crucial for the company's growth plans, as highlighted in its recent prospectus. The vehicle is central to SpaceX's long-term ambitions.

title: The internet was ‘too expensive’ too
summary: The article discusses how early infrastructure platforms often seem economically unfeasible due to high costs, immature supply chains, and lack of scaling. It draws a parallel with the internet's early days to illustrate this point.

title: CEO Series: Open Cosmos’ Rafel Jorda Siquier on finding a niche in space.
summary: Open Cosmos' CEO Rafel Jorda Siquier discusses combining Earth observation, connectivity, and IoT into a single platform in an interview with SpaceNews. The conversation highlights the company's strategy to find a niche in the space industry.

title: LatConnect 60 announces accelerated growth investment round to build AUKUS-aligned Highest Resolution SWIR Satellite constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 (LC60) is accelerating its growth investment round to develop a high-resolution SWIR satellite constellation aligned with AUKUS. The Australian Earth observation and AI company aims to expand its capabilities with this new funding.

title: Space Force eyes 2027 demonstrations of in-space refueling and satellite servicing
summary: The U.S. Space Force plans to conduct in-space refueling and satellite servicing demonstrations in 2027. The USSF-23 mission will launch vehicles to showcase these technologies in geostationary orbit.

title: SpaceX files for IPO
summary: SpaceX has filed documents for an initial public offering (IPO) on May 20, revealing its financial details and ambitious plans. This marks a significant step toward going public for the private space company.

title: U.S. ‘more prepared’ for next WRC
summary: White House officials state they are better prepared for the next World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC) to defend U.S. radio-frequency spectrum priorities. The conference is a major international event focused on spectrum allocation.

title: SpaceX's 1st Starship V3 megarocket launch scrubbed at last minute. Sorry, Nicki Minaj
summary: SpaceX's first Starship V3 megarocket launch was scrubbed at the last minute due to technical issues. The planned inaugural flight on May 21 did not proceed as scheduled.

title: Watch Rocket Lab launch private Japanese Earth-observing satellite early on May 22
summary: Rocket Lab is set to launch a private Japanese Earth-observing radar satellite for Synspective on May 22, with live coverage available. This mission highlights the growing collaboration between U.S. launch providers and Japanese satellite companies.

title: Epic 'Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu' merch has landed. 'This is the way' to Grogu Crocs, Lego & more
summary: Star Wars: The Mandalorian and Grogu merchandise has been released, featuring items like Grogu Crocs and Lego sets. The launch coincides with the new film's release in theaters.

title: This exoplanet weather forecast by the James Webb Space Telescope calls for sandy skies and a clear (alien) sunset
summary: Astronomers used the James Webb Space Telescope to observe weather changes on a distant exoplanet, noting a cloudy morning transitioning to clear skies by dusk. This marks a significant advancement in exoplanet atmospheric studies.

title: 'Stop lying!' Conspiracy theorist confronts Artemis 2 astronauts, accuses them of faking their moon mission
summary: A conspiracy theorist confronted Artemis 2 astronauts on Capitol Hill, accusing them of faking their moon mission. The astronauts showed more restraint than Buzz Aldrin did in a similar situation.

title: Nikon Action 16x50 binoculars review
summary: Nikon's new Action 16x50 binoculars were tested for stargazing, revealing lunar craters and Orion's belt with sharp contrast.

title: Who is Embo, the enigmatic villain in 'The Mandalorian & Grogu'?
summary: The article discusses the character Embo, a villain from 'The Mandalorian & Grogu' movie, who is not a newcomer to Star Wars lore despite being mysterious to casual fans.

title: Lunar Outpost has big plans for the moon. The new Pegasus lunar rover is just the start
summary: Lunar Outpost has unveiled designs for the new Pegasus lunar rover, planned for a 2028 launch. The Colorado-based company has ambitious plans beyond this initial mission.

title: Strange stacked stones spotted on Mars | Space photo of the day for May 21, 2026
summary: NASA's Perseverance Mars rover discovered an unusual stack of rocks on the Martian surface. The formation has sparked curiosity among scientists and space enthusiasts.

title: Starship Flight 12 Scrubs Due to Ship Quick Disconnect Issues
summary: Starship Flight 12 was scrubbed due to issues with the ship's quick disconnect mechanism. This marks a setback in SpaceX's testing timeline for the Starship program.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon 9, Electron, and Vega C launch missions alongside Starship Flight 12
summary: Six space launches are scheduled worldwide in the next week, including SpaceX's Falcon 9, Rocket Lab's Electron, Arianespace's Vega C, and SpaceX's Starship Flight 12.

title: Vega C launches SMILE to study Earth’s magnetosphere
summary: ESA's SMILE mission launched aboard Arianespace's Vega C rocket to study Earth's magnetosphere. The mission aims to investigate the interaction between solar wind and Earth's magnetic field.

title: Super Heavy Block 3 the Booster of the Future
summary: The article discusses the evolution of the Super Heavy Block 3 booster, the first stage of Starship, highlighting its ongoing improvements. It is presented as a key development in SpaceX's future launch capabilities.

title: Artemis III deep into processing and planning for key HLS test
summary: NASA is advancing planning for Artemis III, a critical crewed mission to the Moon, with deep processing underway. The mission includes a key Human Landing System (HLS) test.

title: Flight 12 readies for the debut SpaceX’s next Ship evolution
summary: SpaceX is preparing for Flight 12, marking the debut of the next evolution of Starship's second stage. The vehicle has undergone multiple iterations since the early test flights of SN8.

title: Rocket Lab signs new launch contracts and acquires robotics company during Q1 2026
summary: Rocket Lab achieved record-breaking performance in Q1 2026 and expanded its operations by signing new launch contracts and acquiring a robotics company.

title: SpaceX’s new Starship pad readies for first launch
summary: SpaceX has completed construction of a redesigned launch pad for its Starship rocket. The new pad is now preparing for its first launch.

title: SpaceX CRS-34 launches to ISS on third attempt
summary: SpaceX successfully launched the CRS-34 mission to the ISS on its third attempt, following a previous docking by Progress MS-34. The mission marks another resupply effort to the International Space Station.

title: Launch Preview: SpaceX and Chinese missions fill busy launch manifest
summary: SpaceX and China have a busy launch schedule with nine launches planned across the United States and China during the week of May 11. The missions span various sectors, highlighting significant activity in the space industry.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on an unspecified space industry news. No specific details about the content were provided.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: Stay space chemical compliant in the EU
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will host a free REACH workshop on chemical compliance in the space sector on 2 June at its technical centre in the Netherlands, with an online participation option available.

title: The young minds space-proofing ESA’s missions
summary: Young researchers are helping the European Space Agency (ESA) test and develop materials to withstand the harsh conditions of space. Their work supports the success of ESA's missions by ensuring spacecraft components are space-proofed.

title: Extended Reality at ESA opens new pathways for space exploration
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) is leveraging Extended Reality (XR) for training, operations, simulations, and public engagement in space exploration. This initiative aims to enhance efficiency and accessibility in space missions.

title: The great parachute bake-out
summary: A space industry event involving a parachute test or deployment, referred to as 'The great parachute bake-out'.

title: Baking a parachute for Mars
summary: The ESA is sterilizing a 35-meter parachute for the ExoMars Rosalind Franklin rover mission to ensure it is 10,000 times cleaner than a smartphone, preventing microbial contamination of Mars. The mission, launching in 2028, aims to search for signs of past or present life on Mars while adhering to strict planetary protection protocols.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has successfully completed space-like tests, remaining on track for a 2027 launch to search for terrestrial planets.

title: Better Moon-Tech Tops NASA Stakeholder Wishlist
summary: NASA's stakeholder survey revealed that improved lunar technology is the top priority for space tech investments. The survey, conducted two years ago, aimed to identify key technical challenges in the space community.

title: ESA Approves Two Scout Science Missions
summary: ESA has approved two Scout science missions aimed at conducting groundbreaking research at a lower cost. The missions demonstrate Europe's commitment to cost-effective scientific exploration.

title: SpaceX Reveals Financial Data Ahead of IPO
summary: SpaceX disclosed $18.67 billion in revenue for 2025 in SEC filings ahead of its upcoming IPO, expected next month.

title: Dems Warn Golden Dome Will Accelerate the Nuclear Arms Race
summary: Democratic lawmakers argue that the Golden Dome missile defense system could escalate the nuclear arms race. The system is designed to protect the U.S. but may provoke adversaries.

title: NASA-Funded Research May Create Chinese Security Risk, Report Says
summary: A Congressional report suggests NASA-funded research since 2015 may involve Chinese researchers, posing a potential security risk. The findings highlight concerns over collaborations with China in U.S. space projects.

title: From Design Reviews to Decision Intelligence: CoLab Rewires Engineering Workflows
summary: CoLab is transforming engineering workflows by improving the decision-making process in design reviews to enhance efficiency and reduce complexity. The company aims to streamline engineering production by addressing inefficiencies in how design decisions are made.

title: CSF Forecasts Launch Supply Running Low
summary: The Commercial Spaceflight Federation (CSF) forecasts a potential shortage in launch supply, with annual satellite launches ranging from 6,000 to 230,000, requiring up to 7,000 launches per year depending on future scenarios.

title: Vast Launches Satellite Bus Business Line
summary: Vast, a commercial space station company, has launched a new satellite bus business line to sell satellite buses targeting low-cost, high-volume, and high-power missions across communications, Earth observation, and national security programs.

title: York Space Systems to Acquire Solestial
summary: York Space Systems is acquiring Solestial to expand its capabilities in space technology. This acquisition marks a significant move for York Space Systems' M&A team in 2024.

title: Astrolab Unveils Payloads Flying on FLIP Lunar Mission
summary: Astrolab has announced the payloads that will fly on its upcoming FLIP lunar mission, scheduled for this year. The mission will carry a variety of scientific payloads to the Moon aboard a rover.

title: LatConnect 60 Looks to Raise Up to $20M in Growth Round to Fund SWIR Constellation
summary: LatConnect 60 is seeking to raise between $5 million and $20 million in a growth round to fund its short-wave infrared (SWIR) satellite constellation. The company aims to expand its satellite network for enhanced Earth observation capabilities.

title: SSC and SpaceWERX to Launch Orbital Logistics Challenge
summary: The Space Force’s Space Systems Command (SSC) and SpaceWERX are launching an In-Domain Orbital Logistics Challenge to advance commercial technologies for satellite sustainment. The initiative aims to identify and develop innovative solutions to support satellite operations in orbit.

title: Planet is Building an Agentic Geospatial AI Query
summary: Planet is developing an agentic AI geospatial query system that allows users to ask natural language questions about geospatial data, such as identifying new construction sites in Florida. This technology aims to enhance accessibility and usability of satellite imagery and data.

title: Amadeus IT Group to Use Spire’s Aircraft Tracking Data for Airport Management Tool
summary: Amadeus IT Group will integrate Spire Global's aircraft tracking data into its cloud platform for airport management. This collaboration aims to enhance airport operations using Spire's aviation intelligence.

title: SpaceX’s IPO Filing Gives First Look Into Company’s Financials
summary: SpaceX has filed initial documents for an IPO, providing the first public glimpse into its financials. This marks a significant step for the company as it prepares to go public.

title: China’s Home Field Advantage at WRC-27 Worries US Operators
summary: China's hosting of the 2027 World Radiocommunication Conference (WRC-27) is raising concerns among U.S. space operators due to its potential influence on critical satellite agenda items. The event is expected to significantly impact the global space sector.

title: Redwire Taps Voyager for Subcontract for DARPA Otter Mission
summary: Redwire has awarded Voyager Technologies a subcontract for DARPA's Otter mission to supply a high-precision Acceleration Measurement System (AMS). The mission aims to advance space technology for defense applications.

title: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon CEOs Want Multiple Constellations for D2D Service
summary: AT&T, T-Mobile, and Verizon CEOs discussed their satellite strategies for direct-to-device (D2D) services at an investor event, emphasizing the need for multiple satellite constellations to support this initiative.

title: Iceye Launches New Solution for Banking Sector
summary: Iceye has launched a new natural catastrophe monitoring solution targeting the international banking sector. The offering aims to provide critical data for risk assessment and decision-making in the financial industry.

title: HackerOne Exec Explains Key Security Trends in the Age of AI
summary: Generative AI is being used as a new weapon by hackers, posing significant cybersecurity challenges. HackerOne, a key player in cybersecurity, is addressing these emerging threats.
